title: aspidistra paucitepala subsp. paucitepala
intro: Aspidistra paucitepala subsp. paucitepala, a rare and lesser-known species of the Aspidistra genus, is a type of flowering plant commonly known as the 'Taitung Aspidistra' or 'Taitung Aspidistra subsp. paucitepala'. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which are used in traditional medicine in Taiwan to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in antioxidants and have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and protecting against infections. Traditionally, the roots of Aspidistra paucitepala subsp. paucitepala are prepared as a tea or infusion, while the leaves are sometimes used in herbal preparations such as infusions or decoctions.
